research_answer: Uganda's export diversity profile reveals something important about Sub-Saharan Africa as a whole. Globally, Uganda's diversity is low — it exports a relatively narrow range of products. But when compared only to other Sub-Saharan African countries, it looks average. This means that low export diversity is the regional norm in SSA, not a Uganda-specific anomaly. Combined with the fact that Uganda's products are fairly ubiquitous — meaning they are produced by many countries and therefore tend to be less complex — this paints a picture of a region where most exporters are clustered in the same peripheral, low-complexity product space. The broader implication is that any SSA country seeking to stand out globally must break from regional norms by moving into more complex, less ubiquitous product categories.
supporting_quotes: Uganda places in the upper left of the distribution; its diversity is fairly low compared to all countries, but average when compared to other SSA countries (shown in orange).
And in line with our theory, the products that Uganda produces are fairly ubiquitous.
